NCT02878330.name: Nirsevimab Phase 2b
NCT02878330.group: Healthy preterm infants (29-35 wk GA), nirsevimab 50 mg IM single dose vs placebo (Day 150)
NCT02878330.snippet: Source: Griffin MP et al. N Engl J Med 2020;383:415-425 (Nirsevimab Phase 2b).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 1,453 healthy preterm infants born between 29 weeks 0 days and 34 weeks 6 days gestational age randomized 2:1 double-blind to a single intramuscular injection of nirsevimab 50 mg (n=969) or placebo (n=484) at the start of the RSV season. Followed for 150 days after dose.

evidence.label: Primary - Medically Attended RSV-LRTI through Day 150
evidence.text: Medically attended RSV-associated LRTI through Day 150: nirsevimab 25/969 (2.6%) vs placebo 46/484 (9.5%); relative risk 0.30 (95% CI 0.19 to 0.48; P<0.001). Vaccine efficacy 70.1% (95% CI 52.3 to 81.2). Primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Secondary - RSV-Hospitalisation
evidence.text: RSV-LRTI hospitalisation through Day 150: nirsevimab 8/969 (0.8%) vs placebo 20/484 (4.1%); VE 78.4%. Relative risk approximately 0.20 (95% CI 0.09-0.46).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR stratified by hemisphere and gestational age. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo IM. D3 LOW - 96% primary follow-up. D4 LOW - central PCR confirmation + blinded adjudication of LRTI. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; max age 365 Days; healthy volunteers: t. CRITERIA: "Key Inclusion Criteria:~1. Healthy infants born between 29 weeks 0 days and 34 weeks 6 days GA.~2. Infants who are entering their first full RSV season at the time of screening.~Key Exclusion Criteria:~1. Meets American Academy of Pediatrics (AAP) or other local criteria to receive commercial palivizumab.~2. Any fever (>= 100.4°F [>= 38.0°C], regardless of route) or lower respiratory illness within 7 days prior to randomization.~3. Acute illness (defined as the presence of moderate or sever...

NCT03979313.name: MELODY
NCT03979313.group: Healthy late-preterm and term infants (>=35 wk GA), nirsevimab 50 or 100 mg IM single dose vs placebo (Day 150)
NCT03979313.snippet: Source: Hammitt LL et al. N Engl J Med 2022;386:837-846 (MELODY).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 1,490 healthy late-preterm and term infants (>=35 weeks gestational age) randomized 2:1 double-blind to a single intramuscular injection of nirsevimab 50 mg (<5 kg) or 100 mg (>=5 kg) (n=994) or placebo (n=496) before the RSV season and followed for 150 days.

evidence.label: Primary - Medically Attended RSV-LRTI through Day 150
evidence.text: Medically attended RSV-associated LRTI through Day 150: nirsevimab 12/994 (1.2%) vs placebo 25/496 (5.0%); relative risk 0.25 (95% CI 0.12 to 0.51; P<0.001). Vaccine efficacy 74.5% (95% CI 49.6 to 87.1). Primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Secondary - Hospitalisation and Safety
evidence.text: RSV-LRTI hospitalisation: nirsevimab 6/994 (0.6%) vs placebo 8/496 (1.6%); VE 62.1%. Grade 3+ adverse events balanced; no anaphylaxis. Injection-site reactions minimal.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR stratified by hemisphere and baseline weight band. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo IM. D3 LOW - 97% primary follow-up. D4 LOW - central PCR confirmation + blinded adjudication of LRTI. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 0 Years; max age 1 Year; healthy volunteers: t. CRITERIA: "Key Inclusion Criteria:~* Healthy infants in their first year of life and born at or after 35 weeks 0 days GA~* Infants who are entering their first RSV season at the time of screening~Key Exclusion Criteria:~* Meets national or other local criteria to receive commercial palivizumab~* Any fever (≥ 100.4°F [≥ 38.0°C], regardless of route) or acute illness within 7 days prior to randomization~* Active RSV infection (a child with signs/symptoms of respiratory infection must have negative RSV tes...

NCT05437510.name: HARMONIE
NCT05437510.group: Infants <=12 mo entering first RSV season, nirsevimab single dose vs no intervention (open-label effectiveness)

allOutcomes.title: RSV-LRTI hospitalisation through first RSV season (primary)
Very severe RSV-LRTI through first RSV season

NCT05437510.snippet: Source: Drysdale SB et al. N Engl J Med 2024;388:2425-2435 (HARMONIE).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 8,058 infants 12 months of age or younger entering their first RSV season across France, Germany, and the UK randomized 1:1 open-label pragmatic design to a single intramuscular injection of nirsevimab (n=4,037) or no intervention (standard-of-care; n=4,021). Primary effectiveness analysis covered one RSV season.

evidence.label: Primary - RSV-LRTI Hospitalisation through First RSV Season
evidence.text: RSV-LRTI hospitalisation through one RSV season: nirsevimab 11/4,037 (0.27%) vs no intervention 60/4,021 (1.49%); relative risk 0.17 (95% CI 0.09 to 0.31). Vaccine effectiveness 83.2% (95% CI 67.8 to 92.0). Primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Secondary - Very Severe RSV-LRTI
evidence.text: Very severe RSV-LRTI hospitalisation (ICU admission or supplemental oxygen >=3 days): nirsevimab 5/4,037 vs no intervention 19/4,021; VE 75.7% (95% CI 32.8-90.9). RR 0.26 (95% CI 0.10-0.70).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central randomization. D2 SOME CONCERNS - open-label pragmatic design (no placebo; effectiveness study). D3 LOW - 98% follow-up. D4 LOW - RSV hospitalisation centrally PCR-confirmed. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 0 Days; max age 12 Months; healthy volunteers: t. title: RapidMeta Infectious Disease | Nirsevimab for Infant RSV Lower Respiratory Tract Infection Prevention v1.1
